name: United States
welcome: Hello! Welcome to the United States of America — a huge country full of adventure, nature, and amazing cities!
summary: The United States is one of the largest countries in the world.
It has 50 states, from the icy wilderness of Alaska to the sunny beaches of Hawaii.
The USA is famous for its national parks, diverse cities, and innovations that changed the world.

LOCATION.intro: The USA stretches across an enormous piece of North America!

items.title: USA in North America
narration: The United States covers most of the middle section of North America.
It stretches from the Atlantic Ocean on the east to the Pacific Ocean on the west.
It also includes Alaska up near the Arctic and the tropical islands of Hawaii.

LANDMARKS.intro: The USA has famous landmarks from coast to coast!

items.title: Statue of Liberty
narration: The Statue of Liberty stands on an island in New York Harbour, welcoming people to America!
It was a gift from France to the United States in 1886.
Lady Liberty holds a torch of freedom and a tablet with an important date.

items.title: Grand Canyon
narration: The Grand Canyon is one of the greatest natural wonders on Earth!
It is 446 kilometres long and over a kilometre deep.
The Colorado River carved it out over millions of years of flowing water.

items.title: Mount Rushmore
narration: Mount Rushmore has four US presidents carved right into the mountain rock!
The faces of George Washington, Thomas Jefferson, Theodore Roosevelt, and Abraham Lincoln look out over the Black Hills.
Each face is about 18 metres tall — taller than a five-story building!

NATURE.intro: The USA has some of the world's most stunning natural scenery!

items.title: Yellowstone
narration: Yellowstone is the world's first national park — created to protect nature forever.
Its rainbow-coloured hot springs and geysers are like something from another planet.
The famous Old Faithful geyser shoots boiling water into the air every 90 minutes!

items.title: Niagara Falls
narration: Niagara Falls is one of the world's most powerful waterfalls!
More than 3,000 tonnes of water flow over its edge every second.
You can ride a boat right up to the edge of the falls and feel the incredible mist!

FOOD.intro: American food comes from cultures all around the world!

items.title: Hamburger
narration: The hamburger is America's most famous food — enjoyed all around the world!
A juicy beef patty sits between a soft bun with cheese, lettuce, and tomato.
Americans eat about 50 billion hamburgers every year — that's three a day for everyone!

items.title: Apple Pie
narration: Apple pie is so beloved in America, people say it is as American as apple pie!
It is a golden, flaky pastry filled with sweet cinnamon apples.
Best served warm with a scoop of vanilla ice cream on top — yum!

CITIES.intro: The USA has some of the world's most exciting cities!

items.title: New York City
narration: New York City is one of the greatest cities in the world!
Its famous skyline is filled with towering skyscrapers.
The city never sleeps — it is always buzzing with activity, day and night.

FUN_FACTS.intro: Here are some amazing facts about the USA!

items.title: First on the Moon
narration: In 1969, American astronaut Neil Armstrong became the first human to walk on the Moon!
He said the famous words: One small step for man, one giant leap for mankind.
NASA — the American space agency — has led humans further into space than any other country.

items.title: 50 States
narration: The United States has 50 states — each one is like its own mini-country!
Texas is so big that it could fit 10 United Kingdoms inside it.
Hawaii is the only US state made entirely of islands in the Pacific Ocean.
